name: Southside

spec.method_detail: Muddle mint gently with syrup. Add gin and lime. Shake hard with ice. Double strain into chilled coupe.

history: A gin Mojito served up — that's essentially what the Southside is. Contested origins: some attribute it to the 21 Club in New York; others to Prohibition-era Chicago's South Side gang members who used mint and lime to mask the taste of bathtub gin. Either way, it's a classic gin sour with mint, served up.
